message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Springs)
Cuisine/type signal: Jewish deli / bakery
Why it surfaced in discovery: Established 1963 by Sherman Harris; longtime independent Jewish deli, multi-location (Palm Springs + Palm Desert); standard inclusion on 'historic / longest-operating' lists.
Discovery sources: https://visitpalmsprings.com/blog/post/3-longest-operating-restaurants-in-palm-springs/ | https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/blog/post/historic-restaurants-in-greater-palm-springs/ | https://www.opentable.com/features/historic-restaurants-palm-springs-ca

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Sherman's Deli & Bakery in Palm Springs:

## History

Sherman's was established in 1963 and has become a landmark in the desert. The family-owned hot spot got its start when Wisconsin native Sherman Harris moved to Palm Springs in the 1950s and began working as a line cook after serving in the Army in five major battles. The restaurant is still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet.

## Notable Recognition

It was named Restaurant of the Year and voted No. 1 Sandwich in the Desert by the California State Senate and Assembly, the Supervisors of Riverside County, and the Palm Springs Chamber of Commerce, and was featured in 2017 on Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives.

## Reviews Overview

Sherman's receives strong reviews across multiple platforms. Reviewers note that everything about Sherman's is excellent, with phenomenal food and portions and desserts that are out of this world. It's described as a classic NYC-deli that has not changed at all in the decades it has been patronized. 

Popular menu items mentioned in reviews include pastrami sandwiches, corned beef sandwiches, matzo ball soup, and various baked goods from the on-site bakery.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

# Sherman's Deli & Bakery - Key Information

## Locations
- **Palm Springs:** 401 E. Tahquitz Canyon Way | 760-325-1199
- **Palm Desert:** 73161 Country Club Drive | 760-568-1350

## Hours
"We're open daily 8am to 9pm"

## Menu Structure
The restaurant offers comprehensive dining categories:
- Breakfast and brunch items (featuring fresh-baked cinnamon bread French toast and 8-egg omelets)
- "Famous Sandwich Board" with 40+ options, including their signature hot corned beef on rye
- Dinner selections (rotisserie chicken, roast brisket, BBQ ribs)
- Daily specials featuring Southern-style fried chicken
- Full beverage service (wine and beer)

All dinners include soup or salad, potato options, and vegetables.

## About & History
Sherman's operates as "a landmark in the desert" established in 1963. Currently "owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet," the venue maintains "a New York-style family restaurant" concept with fresh daily bakery items and an extensive deli menu.

Notable: Featured on Food Network's *Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives* with Guy Fieri.

## Contact & Additional Info
- Online ordering available
- BBQ sauce available for purchase ($7.95)
- Early bird dining specials (4pm-6pm year-round)
- Social media: Facebook and Instagram presence

No schema.org markup detected in provided content.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast deli sandwich spots in Palm Springs:

## Top Recommendations:

**Sherman's Deli & Bakery** - A NYC-style family restaurant that has been operating since 1963. It's known for its delicious sandwiches and breakfast options, as well as its tempting pastries and cakes. Their Famous Sandwich Board offers more than 40 favorites including Hot Corned Beef piled high on rye bread served with Potato Salad or Cole Slaw.

**Townie Bagels** - Hands-down one of the best stops in Palm Springs for a real-deal breakfast. For a breakfast sandwich, try an Everything bagel with egg, bacon, and cheddar.

**Cheeky's Palm Springs** - A modern eatery known for its creative breakfast and lunch sandwiches, bacon flights, and specialty cinnamon rolls.

These establishments consistently receive praise for their quality breakfast sandwich offerings and are popular with both locals and visitors exploring Palm Springs' dining scene.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best Jewish delis in the Palm Springs/Coachella Valley area:

## Top Recommendations:

**Manhattan in the Desert** - This is a genuine New York-style Jewish deli specializing in lox, homemade soups, hot dogs and sandwiches, which are all served "mile-high," stacked to perfection and with a free sampling of delicious pickled goods. According to reviews, if you want the best Jewish Deli, go to Manhattan in the Desert, as the food is terrific.

**Deli Chick** - Deli Chick has the best sandwiches in the Coachella area with a clean, casual and friendly atmosphere.

**Sherman's Deli & Bakery** - This is the only kosher-style deli with a bakery in Palm Springs. Founded by Sherman Harris in 1953, this spot is a Palm Springs institution, and the eatery's signature Club is hands-down one of the best sandwiches anywhere.

**J's Deli** - J's Deli is owned by Jay and Vanessa Rubinstein and opened in April 2022 in a space that was previously the Manhattan deli.

**On The Mark** - This newer addition to the Coachella Valley restaurant scene became an area staple and THE premier spot for sandwiches in the desert, offering hand-crafted hoagies, artisanal coffees, and fresh cuts of meat.

Links: [{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery (Palm Springs, CA) on Diners, Drive-Ins & Dives","url":"https://www.dinersdriveinsdiveslocations.com/shermans-deli-bakery-california.html"},{"title":"Shermans Deli and Bakery Palm Springs Bakeries Delis Cafes","url":"https://directory.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/shermans-deli-and-bakery-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli Palm Springs | A Diners, Drive-ins, and Dives Recap - Cactus Hugs","url":"https://cactushugs.com/shermans-deli-palm-springs-diners-drive-ins-dives/"},{"title":"Family Ties | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/shermans-deli-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery","url":"https://shermansdeli.com/"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery - Wikipedia","url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sherman's_Deli_&_Bakery"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery in Palm Springs, California | TVFoodMaps","url":"https://www.tvfoodmaps.com/restaurant/CA/Palm-Springs/Shermans-Deli-Bakery"},{"title":"SHERMAN'S DELI, Palm Desert - Restaurant Reviews, Photos & Phone Number - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32846-d535463-Reviews-Sherman_s_Deli-Palm_Desert_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Sherman's Deli & Bakery - Palm Springs & Palm Desert, California - Gil's Thrilling (And Filling) Blog","url":"https://www.nmgastronome.com/?p=44840"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sherman's Deli in Palm Springs and its connection to Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives:

Sherman's Deli & Bakery is a pair of kosher delicatessens in Palm Springs and Palm Desert, California, and the Palm Springs location has appeared on the Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives.

The restaurant was featured in 2017 on Diners, Drive-Ins, and Dives, and Guy Fieri visited Sherman's Deli and Bakery in Palm Desert (near Palm Springs) for his Food Network show. Guy gave the deli high praise, calling Sherman's a "gem" and "outstanding."

Beyond the TV appearance, Sherman's was named Restaurant of the Year and voted No. 1 Sandwich in the Desert by the California State Senate and Assembly, the Supervisors of Riverside County, and the Palm Springs Chamber of Commerce.

The family-owned hot spot got its start when Wisconsin native Sherman Harris moved to Palm Springs in the 1950s, and it is still owned and operated by Sherman Harris' son, Sam and daughter Janet.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best restaurants in the Coachella Valley and historic Palm Springs:

## Historic Palm Springs Restaurants

Le Vallauris in Palm Springs offers classic French cuisine in a romantic setting, located in a historic building with a charming outdoor patio surrounded by lush greenery.

Copley's On Palm Canyon is housed in a historic property that was once Cary Grant's estate, serving elegant fare like pan-seared jumbo scallops with white truffle and braised Berkshire pork belly.

Eight4Nine is located in a historic building—the city's first post office—and features chic New American cuisine in a contemporary atmosphere.

## Other Notable Coachella Valley Restaurants

Keedy's, voted the best diner in the Coachella Valley, puts a fresh, modern spin on the original 1957 Palm Desert favorite, offering house-made waffles, juicy burgers, hearty omelets, crisp salads, and savory sandwiches.

Chez Pierre Bistro in Palm Desert offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

The Edge Steakhouse at The Ritz-Carlton in Rancho Mirage specializes in prime steaks and fresh seafood with floor-to-ceiling windows providing stunning views of the Coachella Valley.

Morgan's in the Desert at La Quinta Resort & Club offers refined farm-to-table cuisine inspired by the flavors of the American Southwest.

content.text:
I have enough to build the assessment. Key findings:

- Schema markup: Yoast-generated WebPage/WebSite + OpenGraph only. NO Restaurant schema, NO LocalBusiness, NO hours, address, or menu structured data. Generic CMS-default markup.
- Tripadvisor: 3,598 reviews, 4.4 stars, Travelers' Choice 2025, ranked #2 of 18 "Dessert Spots" (a quirky category placement)
- Yelp PS location: 4,127 reviews, 5,123 photos; Palm Desert: 2,369 reviews
- Founded 1963 by Sherman Harris; family-owned by son Sam and daughter Janet
- D,D&D feature 2017 (verified across multiple sources)
- Two locations: Palm Springs (Tahquitz Canyon Way) + Palm Desert (Country Club Dr)
- Direct competitors: Manhattan in the Desert, J's Deli, Deli Chick, On The Mark

Now I'll submit the structured output.

input.name: Sherman's Deli & Bakery
input.corridor: Downtown Palm Springs (401 E Tahquitz Canyon Way); second location on Country Club Drive in north Palm Desert

reputation.review_volume: Yelp Palm Springs ~4,100+ and Palm Desert ~2,300+; Tripadvisor PS ~3,600 — combined 10,000+ across platforms
reputation.sentiment: mostly positive with mixed pricing complaints
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews continue to praise pastrami, corned beef, matzo ball soup, and bakery items; recurring critiques cite high prices (e.g., reports of $25 bagel-and-lox) and occasional inconsistency. Tripadvisor Travelers' Choice 2025 badge indicates sustained recent positive volume.
reputation.longevity: Operating since 1963 (63 years); continuously family-owned, currently run by founder Sherman Harris's son Sam and daughter Janet
credentials: Featured on Food Network's Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives (2017)
Standard inclusion on Visit Palm Springs / Visit Greater Palm Springs 'historic restaurants' lists

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best Jewish deli Palm Springs', 'historic restaurants Palm Springs', 'best sandwich Palm Springs', and 'best breakfast Palm Springs deli'. Does NOT typically surface for broad 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' or 'fine dining' queries (correct exclusion — it's a deli, not fine dining). Listed by Visit Greater Palm Springs and Palm Springs Life directory.
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate — described as a New York-style / Jewish deli landmark since 1963, family-owned, known for sandwiches and bakery. Minor recurring drift: occasionally called 'kosher' (it is kosher-style, not certified kosher) and one source dated founding to 1953 instead of 1963.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Site has Yoast-generated WebPage, WebSite, and BreadcrumbList JSON-LD plus OpenGraph tags — but NO Restaurant or LocalBusiness schema, NO structured hours, NO structured address, NO menu schema. Hours and addresses are visible only as plain HTML text. This is generic WordPress/Yoast default markup, not restaurant-optimized structured data. Agent-readability for hours/location/menu is weak.

competitors: J's Deli (Palm Desert — opened 2022 in former Manhattan space)
Keedy's (Palm Desert — historic diner, adjacent category)
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — multi-decade anchor of the Jewish-deli / classic-American breakfast category across both downtown Palm Springs and north Palm Desert corridors; reliably referenced as one of the valley's longest-operating independent restaurants
query_wins: best sandwich Palm Springs / Coachella Valley

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owns and operates shermansdeli.com (WordPress with Yoast SEO plugin — indicates light SEO investment); active Facebook and Instagram; online ordering integrated; paid Palm Springs Life directory listing; merchandise (BBQ sauce) for sale; standalone domain rather than third-party only
convertibility.reasoning: Sherman's is family-owned by the Harris family across two generations with a reachable local decision-maker (Sam and Janet Harris). Evidence of marketing spend is moderate but real: a maintained website with SEO plugin, active social, paid local directory presence, online ordering, and a branded retail product. The schema gap on the site is a concrete, sellable fix — they already invested in a CMS and Yoast, so adding Restaurant/LocalBusiness schema, structured hours, and menu markup is an incremental upgrade rather than a rebuild. Strong reputation plus a clear technical gap on their own domain makes this a textbook convertible profile.

flags: [UNVERIFIED] Claim that Sherman's was 'named Restaurant of the Year and voted No. 1 Sandwich in the Desert by the California State Senate and Assembly, the Supervisors of Riverside County, and the Palm Springs Chamber of Commerce' appears in search summaries and own-marketing but no primary award citation or year was located — treat as self-reported until a primary source is confirmed

[UNVERIFIED] Exact current Google Maps review count not directly retrieved (Yelp/Tripadvisor counts verified instead); Google review band is inferred to be in the multi-thousands but not directly observed

One source (allaboutdelis.com) lists founding year as 1953 — conflicts with the consistently cited 1963 founding date used by the restaurant itself, Wikipedia, and Visit Palm Springs; 1963 is treated as authoritative
Often described as 'kosher' in third-party summaries; the operation is kosher-style, not certified kosher — minor description drift in the agent surface
